newsSince: Marvell joined the S&P 500 on June 22, 2026 (replacing Pool Corp), a forced-passive-buying event that maximizes ownership visibility. Fundamentals strong but already known: Q1 FY27 record revenue $2.418B (+28% y/y, May 27 beat), raised FY27/FY28 outlook, custom silicon targeted to scale past $10B by FY29, Teralynx T100 102.4Tbps switch debut and 5M+ coherent PICs shipped. Stock +214% YTD, +5% over the window.

sources.label: Marvell S&P 500 inclusion analysis (24/7 Wall St)

verdicts.rankRationale: Residual-alpha vs passives lens: MRVL sits outside the passives/power bottleneck stack and is parked as monitor/benchmark. June 22 S&P 500 inclusion adds forced passive crowding, the opposite of this page's alpha criterion, so it stays at the bottom with a small negative drift.
verdicts.bull: Best-in-class AI interconnect and custom silicon; central AI node with raised FY28 guide.
verdicts.neutral: Strong AI franchise but not a passive/power component.
verdicts.bear: S&P 500 inclusion maxes passive ownership; zero residual alpha for this list.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Maximum crowding; outside the passives bottleneck

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: custom-ASIC/interconnect centrality is intact (Teralynx T100, raised FY28 ~$16.5B guide, 5M+ PICs). But S&P 500 inclusion and a trillion-dollar narrative push visibility beyond the page's 'smaller than megacaps' lens, offsetting the node strength. Net hold one day after the prior up-move.
verdicts.bull: Direct custom-ASIC + 800G/1.6T interconnect node; raised FY27/FY28 guide and T100 switch debut.
verdicts.neutral: Node thesis intact but no longer 'smaller than megacaps' after S&P 500 entry.
verdicts.bear: Qualcomm entering custom ASICs; +214% YTD with maxed visibility erodes the under-the-radar edge.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Custom XPU + scale-out optics/switch ramp toward >$10B by FY29

verdicts.rankRationale: Co-packaged-optics lens: this is MRVL's most direct fit (optical DSPs, CPO, AEC/AOC, 5M+ coherent PICs, NPO/CPO scale-up optics in raised guide). Optical leg strengthened by Q1 beat and T100; Qualcomm-ByteDance headline hits the custom-compute leg, not optics. Small positive on confirmed optics traction.
verdicts.bull: One of the most direct bridges across custom silicon, optical DSPs, CPO and AEC/AOC; 5M+ coherent PICs shipped.
verdicts.neutral: Optics franchise leading but large AI expectations already embedded.
verdicts.bear: +214% YTD; CPO timeline and competition could disappoint lofty expectations.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: NPO/CPO scale-up optics and 1.6T DSP ramp

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list: scale-out networking/CPO/800G-1.6T optics exposure is real but ownership visibility is now maximal. S&P 500 inclusion plus the trillion-dollar narrative keep crowding at a peak for this crowding-aware list; prior down-move already penalized the rerating. Hold near the bottom.
verdicts.bull: Scale-out bandwidth and optics exposure with record FY26 revenue and AI bookings.
verdicts.neutral: Genuine bottleneck content but no hidden alpha after the rerating.
verdicts.bear: +214% YTD and S&P 500 inclusion maximize ownership; opposite of scarce, under-owned exposure.
